evals.prompt: Build a new dynamic-script workflow that takes a URL, uses the saved kimi-k3-browser-agent with Kimi K3 vision/browser tools, and returns the page title plus a one-paragraph summary. Save it through Workflow MCP and tell me where to open it.
evals.expected_output: Calls get_workflow_context first, reads get_workflow_script_spec, writes a script with literal export const meta and meta.input JSON Schema for required URL, uses awaited agent() with the saved agent slug and a structured object schema, validates, saves by name with save_workflow_script, and runs by name. Uses kimi/kimi-k3 (1,048,576 context, max thinking) without retired Kimi/GLM defaults, preserves structured image inputs rather than stringifying screenshot arrays, passes no sessionId to workflow save/run tools, and does not write Postgres directly.

evals.prompt: I have a workflow that runs a coding agent in a workspace, but the live preview returns 404 'Retained sandbox not found' immediately after the run finishes. Fix it.
evals.expected_output: Identifies that `with.keepAfterRun: true` is missing on the workspace/profile step (per references/troubleshooting.md § Workspace / sandbox). Edits the spec to add it. Mentions the persist_workspace_session activity contract and the _should_cleanup_workspaces gate that reads the spec — not just task outputs.

evals.prompt: My durable/run step times out — the agent never seems to start. Walk me through what to check.
evals.expected_output: Triage from references/troubleshooting.md (current agent-sandbox + Kueue model): check the per-session Sandbox + Kueue admission (`kubectl -n workflow-builder get sandbox` and `get workloads` — a Pending/unadmitted Workload means the queue is out of quota or the pod is unschedulable), the per-session pod + daprd sidecar logs, the registry-resolved runtime app id (`runtime_registry.resolve()`), the dapr-agent-py-statestore scope for that app id, and the openshell-sandbox-dapr-webhook namespaceSelector. Distinguishes 'Ignoring unexpected taskCompleted event' (normal replay chatter) from actually stuck. Does NOT mention an AgentRuntime CR phase or an agent-runtime-controller rolling restart — that CRD + Kopf controller are retired.

evals.prompt: I added the Outlook MCP connection to my agent and sent a test message, but the agent never responded. Debug it without exposing any OAuth tokens.
evals.expected_output: Uses references/mcp-connections.md and troubleshooting.md. Checks activepieces-mcp-catalog, ap-microsoft-outlook-service KService readiness and no-port /mcp URL, mcp_connection.connection_external_id -> active app_connection.external_id, the newly launched per-session Sandbox bootstrap, effective allowedTools selection, and runtime [mcp-bootstrap] logs. Explains credentials flow through X-Connection-External-Id/internal decrypt API and avoids printing or embedding OAuth secrets. Does not inspect an AgentRuntime CR, controller, wake annotation, or per-agent Deployment because those are retired.
